history.content:
<thought>
I can see the goal G to the upper-right relative to my position. The cell directly in front of me is floor (.), and moving forward will get me closer toward the goal while avoiding the lava cells.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is straight ahead and slightly above; from my current view, moving forward would step into the open cell below the goal line. The cell directly in front is not lava (#) and should be safe, so I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is directly in front of me (to the upper direction) with no lava in the way. Moving forward should place me on the goal cell.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am adjacent to the goal cell G and my forward path is clear (it is G, not lava). I should move forward to reach it.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I can see G to my right of the current position, while the cell directly in front appears to be floor. I need to turn to align with the goal before moving.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is directly in front of me. The cell ahead is G (safe), so I should move forward to reach it.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
G is no longer directly visible ahead; the cell in front is lava L, so moving forward would be deadly. I should turn to find a safe path around it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I can see the goal G to my right. The cell directly in front is floor '.', which is safe, and moving forward should bring me closer to aligning with the goal route.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is visible directly to the right of my current position. The cell in front is floor '.', not lava or wall, so I should move forward to get in line and then turn toward the goal if needed.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m still at the same relative view; the goal G is to my right, but I cannot see an advance path yet due to partial map rendering. Since the forward cell appears to be floor and safe, I’ll try moving forward to open more of the corridor toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
My local observation hasn’t changed, so I likely hit a wall and didn’t move. The cell in front of me is not usable; I need to reorient. Since the goal is visible to the right, I will turn right to face toward G.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Now I can see the goal G at the left side of my view. Lava L is directly in front of me (below the goal line), so moving forward would be deadly. I should turn left to face toward the goal instead of the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The map view returned to the earlier state, suggesting I turned and now the cell in front is still not safe/doesn’t lead directly. The goal is visible to my right in the current view, so I should turn right again to align toward G.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
In this view, the goal G is immediately to my left (same row). The cell in front of me is lava L, so I must avoid moving forward. I should turn left to face the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The previous move shifted my orientation back so that the goal is now in front of me (G is directly ahead on the visible row). I should move forward toward it; the cell between is not lava and appears as G.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
